healthy.description: The plant appears healthy based on the uploaded leaf image.
organic_treatment: Continue regular watering based on soil moisture.
Keep the plant in suitable sunlight.
Remove dead or damaged leaves regularly.
chemical_treatment: No chemical treatment is required.

grape_black_rot.description: Grape black rot is a fungal disease that causes dark circular spots on grape leaves and can later affect fruits.
organic_treatment: Remove and destroy infected leaves and plant debris.
Prune the plant to improve air circulation.
Avoid overhead watering because wet leaves help fungal spread.
Use neem oil only for mild supportive control, not as a complete cure.
chemical_treatment: Use a grape-safe fungicide recommended for black rot if infection is spreading.
Follow local agricultural guidance and product label instructions carefully.
prevention: Remove fallen infected leaves from the soil.
Inspect leaves regularly during humid or rainy weather.

apple_black_rot.description: Apple black rot is a fungal disease that can cause leaf spots, fruit rot, and cankers on branches.
organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves, fruits, and dead branches.
Destroy infected plant waste instead of composting it.
Improve airflow by proper pruning.
chemical_treatment: Use an apple-safe fungicide if disease spread is severe.
Follow agricultural expert advice and product label instructions.
prevention: Avoid leaving infected fruits under the tree.
Monitor plants during warm and humid conditions.

black_rot.description: Black rot is a serious plant disease that can damage leaves, stems, or fruits depending on the crop.
organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves or plant parts.
Destroy infected crop remains safely.
Avoid spreading water from infected plants to healthy plants.
Improve air circulation around plants.
chemical_treatment: Use crop-specific disease management products only if advised and follow label instructions.

rust.description: Rust disease usually appears as orange, yellow, or brown powder-like spots on leaves.
organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves carefully.
Improve air circulation around the plant.
Apply neem oil spray in mild cases.
Avoid wetting leaves while watering.
chemical_treatment: Use a suitable crop-safe fungicide only if infection is severe and follow product instructions.
prevention: Do not leave infected plant debris near healthy plants.

powdery_mildew.description: Powdery mildew appears as white or gray powder-like patches on leaves.
organic_treatment: Remove heavily infected leaves.
Spray neem oil or a mild baking soda solution carefully.
Place the plant where air circulation is better.
chemical_treatment: Use a recommended crop-safe fungicide if the infection spreads quickly.

leaf_spot.description: Leaf spot disease causes small brown, black, or yellow spots on leaves.
organic_treatment: Remove affected leaves.
Avoid overhead watering.
Use neem oil spray for mild symptoms.
Improve airflow around the plant.
chemical_treatment: Use copper-based or crop-safe fungicide if the disease spreads.
prevention: Water near the soil instead of leaves.

leaf_mold.description: Leaf mold usually appears in humid conditions and causes yellowing or mold-like growth on leaves.
organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves.
Improve ventilation and reduce humidity.
Avoid wetting leaves during watering.
chemical_treatment: Use a crop-safe fungicide if symptoms continue spreading.

early_blight.description: Early blight causes dark circular spots and yellowing, especially on older leaves.
organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves immediately.
Use compost to improve plant strength.
Apply neem oil in early stages.
Avoid splashing soil onto leaves.
chemical_treatment: Use a suitable crop-safe fungicide for blight if symptoms are severe.
prevention: Keep soil from splashing onto leaves.

late_blight.description: Late blight spreads quickly and can damage leaves, stems, and fruits.
organic_treatment: Remove and destroy infected plant parts.
Keep plants dry and well-spaced.
Avoid working with plants when leaves are wet.
chemical_treatment: Use recommended blight fungicide quickly if infection is spreading.
prevention: Monitor plants during cool and wet weather.

bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial spot causes dark water-soaked spots on leaves and fruits.
organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves.
Avoid touching healthy plants after handling infected plants.
Improve spacing and airflow.
chemical_treatment: Copper-based sprays may help reduce spread, but follow local recommendations.

yellow_leaf_curl.description: Yellow leaf curl causes curling, yellowing, and stunted growth, often spread by whiteflies.
organic_treatment: Remove severely infected plants.
Control whiteflies using yellow sticky traps.
Use neem oil to reduce whitefly population.
chemical_treatment: Use recommended insect control only if whitefly infestation is high.

mosaic_virus.description: Mosaic virus causes mottled green-yellow leaf patterns and poor growth.
organic_treatment: Remove infected plants to prevent spread.
Control insects that spread viruses.
Disinfect tools after use.
chemical_treatment: There is no direct chemical cure for viral infection.
prevention: Control aphids and other insect vectors.
Avoid touching healthy plants after infected plants.

spider_mites.description: Spider mites are tiny pests that can cause yellow speckling, webbing, and leaf damage.
organic_treatment: Spray leaves gently with water to reduce mites.
Use neem oil or insecticidal soap in mild cases.
Remove heavily affected leaves.
chemical_treatment: Use a crop-safe miticide only if infestation is severe and follow product instructions.
prevention: Inspect the underside of leaves regularly.

leaf_scorch.description: Leaf scorch causes browning or burning along leaf edges and may be linked to stress, disease, or poor water balance.
organic_treatment: Remove badly damaged leaves.
Check watering consistency.
Protect the plant from extreme heat if needed.
chemical_treatment: Chemical treatment is usually not needed unless a disease is confirmed.
prevention: Reduce heat or drought stress where possible.

citrus_greening.description: Citrus greening is a serious disease that causes yellowing, poor fruit quality, and plant decline.
organic_treatment: Remove severely infected branches if advised.
Control insect vectors such as psyllids.
Keep the plant healthy with proper nutrition.
chemical_treatment: Use expert-recommended insect control for psyllid management if needed.
prevention: Ask local agricultural experts for citrus management guidance.

grape_esca.description: Grape esca, also called black measles, can cause leaf discoloration, internal wood decay, and fruit symptoms.
organic_treatment: Prune infected wood carefully.
Remove severely affected vines if needed.
Improve vineyard sanitation.
chemical_treatment: There is limited chemical cure; ask local agricultural experts for crop-specific management.
prevention: Avoid large pruning wounds where possible.

default.description: The model detected a possible plant disease. Use the prediction as decision support, not a final expert diagnosis.
organic_treatment: Remove badly affected leaves.
Avoid overwatering.
Improve sunlight and air circulation.
Use neem oil spray for mild fungal symptoms.
chemical_treatment: Use crop-specific fungicide or pesticide only if symptoms are severe and follow product instructions.
prevention: Separate infected plants from healthy ones.
